skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Execute a written implementation plan in small batches with review checkpoints, verification, and clear stop conditions.
skill.summary: Guides an AI coding agent through planned implementation work with checkpoints and verification.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static high finding for a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The referenced line is plain skill metadata describing implementation plan execution and contains no cryptographic algorithm, code execution, network access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3, but the line is a natural-language description of the skill workflow. No evidence found of weak cryptography or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected line contains only YAML metadata text about executing implementation plans. There is no code, hash function name, encryption routine, or security-sensitive operation.

content.value_statement: Long implementation plans can lose quality when tasks run without review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through batch execution, verification, and feedback checkpoints.
actual_capabilities: Loads and critically reviews a written implementation plan before starting work.
Executes the first three tasks by default as a manageable batch.
Tracks each task through in-progress and completed states.
Runs the verification steps specified by the plan.
Reports completed work and verification output before continuing.
Stops and asks for help when blockers or repeated verification failures occur.
limitations: Requires a clear written implementation plan to be useful.
Does not define how to create the plan before execution starts.
Depends on user feedback between batches for review decisions.
References a finishing sub-skill that may not be installed in every environment.

use_cases.title: Ship Planned Code Changes
use_cases.description: Execute a prepared development plan in controlled batches with verification after each task.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Progress
use_cases.description: Give an AI coding agent structured checkpoints so implementation progress can be inspected before more work continues.

use_cases.title: Maintain Implementation Discipline
use_cases.description: Keep long coding sessions aligned with the plan, test expectations, and explicit stop conditions.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to implement this plan. Review it first, then complete the first batch and report verification results.

prompt_templates.title: Execute With Review Points
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill for this implementation plan. Stop after each batch, summarize changes, show verification, and wait for feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a Risky Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to review this plan critically before implementation. Raise concerns before coding if steps are unclear or risky.

prompt_templates.title: Complete a Multi-Batch Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to execute this full plan in batches. Track task status, run specified checks, stop on blockers, and use the finishing workflow after all tasks pass.

output_examples.input: Implement the first three tasks from the plan and report back.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, completes the first batch, lists completed work, reports verification results, and says it is ready for feedback.

output_examples.input: Continue after review comments on the previous batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies requested changes, starts the next batch, updates task status, runs checks, and pauses again for review.

output_examples.input: A verification step fails repeatedly during a batch.
output_examples.output: The assistant stops implementation, reports the blocker, explains the failing verification, and asks for clarification instead of guessing.

best_practices: Provide a written plan with small tasks and explicit verification steps.
Review each batch before asking the assistant to continue.
Update the plan when feedback changes the implementation approach.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without a concrete implementation plan.
Forcing progress when dependencies, instructions, or tests are blocked.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI coding agent through a written implementation plan using small batches, verification, and review checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it create implementation plans?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to execute an existing written plan, not to design the plan from scratch.

faq.question: How many tasks does it run at once?
faq.answer: The default first batch is three tasks, then the assistant reports results before continuing.

faq.question: What happens when a task is unclear?
faq.answer: The skill tells the assistant to stop and ask for clarification instead of guessing.

faq.question: Can it help with test failures?
faq.answer: It can run planned verification steps and report failures. Repeated failures should stop the batch for human review.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains workflow guidance only, with no confirmed dangerous code patterns.
